$description: Light-mode semantic color tokens for the shadcn Vega preset (neutral baseColor). Each token references a primitive in primitives.json via DTCG {} alias — Style Dictionary resolves at build time to literal hex values. Aliases match Vega's `shadcn init` output mapped to the closest Tailwind primitive.

background-success.$description: Status surface — success pill fill (e.g. session 'Complete'). Pairs with foreground-success.

foreground-success.$description: Status text — success pill label and positive inline cues ('connection successful', 'key stored'). Pairs with background-success.

background-info.$description: Status surface — info pill fill. Pairs with foreground-info.

foreground-info.$description: Status text — info pill label. Pairs with background-info.

background-warning.$description: Status surface — warning pill fill (e.g. session 'In progress'). Pairs with foreground-warning.

foreground-warning.$description: Status text — warning pill label. Pairs with background-warning.

background-error.$description: Status surface — error pill fill. Pairs with foreground-error. Distinct from background-destructive (solid danger actions).

foreground-error.$description: Status text — error pill label. Pairs with background-error. Distinct from foreground-destructive (inline danger text).

foreground-vigilance-0.$description: Clinical vigilance ramp — uncategorized/stage 0. The one clinical-severity ramp the kit sanctions (CONVENTIONS §6 exception); an 8-step Loomis alertness→sleep scale (0·A1·A2·A3·B1·B2·B3·C). Theme-independent (same primitive in light + dark) so a stage reads the same colour in either theme, matching the V1 clinical strip. Red end caps at 500/600/700 (not 900) so the deepest stages stay legible on the dark muted track.

foreground-label-adaptive.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Adaptive. The second clinical ramp the kit sanctions (CONVENTIONS §6 exception, ruled July 2026): the peak/valley label vocabulary a clinician assigns when marking a spectral fingerprint. Theme-independent (same primitive in light + dark) so a label reads the same colour in either theme. The `foreground-` role is the label's ink — it paints the chip's dot and text AND the peak marker on the spectrum/IC/ERP canvases, which is the point: chip and marker are one source and cannot drift.

foreground-label-ambiguous.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Ambiguous (shared by the peak and valley vocabularies).

foreground-label-monitoring.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Monitoring (shared by the peak and valley vocabularies).

foreground-label-suppressed.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Suppressed. Valley vocabulary: cool/blue so a marked valley reads as 'below the slope', distinct from the warm peak palette.

background-label-adaptive.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Adaptive, fill role. The selected label chip fills with its own colour and carries foreground-white; role purity (§8) requires the fill to be a background- token even though it aliases the same primitive as its foreground- twin.

background-label-rigid.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Rigid, fill role.

background-label-ambiguous.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Ambiguous, fill role.

background-label-monitoring.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Monitoring, fill role.

background-label-suppressed.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Suppressed, fill role.

background-label-expected-low.$description: Clinical annotation-label ramp — Expected-low, fill role.

border-success-subtle.$description: Status stroke — success, surface-equivalent (matches background-success). STROKE_COLOR-scoped counterpart so a status colour can paint a border.

border-primary.$description: Border/stroke on checked or selected (primary-emphasis) controls: checked checkbox box, selected rich-group card, slider thumb. Same primitives as background-primary — created in the PEA-219 border-role correction (a background token must not paint strokes).

border-subtle.$description: Hairline border one step quieter than border-default — the charts grid-line color. Promoted from Figma's unofficial/border-1 (259 Charts grid-line bindings) in the PEA-219 reorg.
